problem.title: RKO-LIO gyro-bias feedback gain on the NCLT 2013-01-10 window
problem.question: How strong should the online gyro-bias feedback be for RKO-LIO's IMU rotation prior on NCLT?

variants.label: Raw gyro prior (gain 0)
variants.intent: Use the raw-gyro rotation prior with no online bias correction.
variants.note: Scan-to-map odometry with IMU rotation prior (GT-seeded init; IMU gyro preintegration used as ICP rotation guess).
variants.readability_note: Adds extra tuning knobs and therefore more command complexity.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stable-interface compatible, but with a larger parameter surface.
variants.decision_reason: Best combined benchmark score on the shared dataset and interface.

variants.label: Bias feedback gain 0.1
variants.note: Scan-to-map odometry with IMU rotation prior (GT-seeded init; IMU gyro preintegration used as ICP rotation guess).
variants.readability_note: Adds extra tuning knobs and therefore more command complexity.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stable-interface compatible, but with a larger parameter surface.
variants.decision_reason: Useful for comparison, but not strong enough to replace the current default.

variants.label: Bias feedback gain 0.3 (default)
variants.intent: Repository default; gyro-bias feedback that lets the IMU prior beat pure-LiDAR ICP on NCLT.
variants.note: Scan-to-map odometry with IMU rotation prior (GT-seeded init; IMU gyro preintegration used as ICP rotation guess).
variants.readability_note: Adds extra tuning knobs and therefore more command complexity.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stable-interface compatible, but with a larger parameter surface.
variants.decision_reason: Useful for comparison, but not strong enough to replace the current default.

variants.label: Bias feedback gain 0.6
variants.intent: Strong correction; checks for over-correction / noise chasing.
variants.note: Scan-to-map odometry with IMU rotation prior (GT-seeded init; IMU gyro preintegration used as ICP rotation guess).
variants.readability_note: Adds extra tuning knobs and therefore more command complexity.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stable-interface compatible, but with a larger parameter surface.
variants.decision_reason: Useful for comparison, but not strong enough to replace the current default.
